title: Pull-Request and Code Review Evidence for Payment Microservices

compliance.assurance_rationale: Auto-migrated from cat-22 markdown on Phase 1.3 run. Requires SME review to confirm clause accuracy, mode (satisfies vs detects-violation-of), and assurance level (contributing/partial/full).

app: Custom HEC `sourcetype="github:pull_request"` or Splunk Add-on for Bitbucket if used
spl:
index=devsecops sourcetype="github:pull_request" earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_payment_repos.csv repo OUTPUT pci_repo
| where pci_repo="true" AND merged="true"
| eval review_ok=if(review_count>=1 AND match(labels,"(?i)security-reviewed"),1,0)
| where review_ok=0
| table _time, repo, user, review_count, labels, url
| sort _time
value: Ingests Git platform webhooks to verify merges to payment repositories include required reviewers, supporting secure software engineering practices for custom payment software.
implementation: (1) Configure org-wide webhooks with HEC token. (2) Align label policy with secure SDLC. (3) Block deploy pipeline on Splunk summary alert if desired. (4) Monthly export for PCI evidence binder.
visualization: Table (exceptions), pie (review_ok ratio overall), timeline (merge times).
knownFalsePositives: Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
